own_review.issue: Requestor-facing 'my requests' endpoints (they take isMyRequest and are used by ordinary requestors to view their own requests) are now gated behind approver-only permissions (APPROVE_TOPICS/APPROVE_TOPICS_CREATE/APPROVE_CONNECTORS/APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS). Regular users with only REQUEST_* permissions will get 401 on their own request lists — a functional/security-model regression introduced by this PR.

Left without @PermissionAllowed and the service method (TopicControllerService.createClaimTopicRequest, line 303) has no auth check either — endpoint is fully unprotected, inconsistent with /createClaimConnectorRequest which got REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS in the same PR.

@Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) is placed on the annotation declaration, where it has no effect on aspect precedence; it must be on the PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor class (or via @Order/Ordered on the aspect bean) to guarantee the permission check runs before other advice/validation.

Migration is incomplete: blocking service-layer checks remain. approveConnectorRequests/declineConnectorRequests still check only APPROVE_CONNECTORS while the controller annotation allows APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS OR APPROVE_CONNECTORS, so a user with only APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS passes the aspect but gets a 200 + NOT_AUTHORIZED body from the service; createConnectorRequest/restartConnector/createConnectorDeleteRequest keep now-redundant checks.
New PermissionConstraintException handler does not log (no log.error), unlike every other handler in this class — denied-access events become invisible in logs.

Extends jakarta.validation.ConstraintDeclarationException, which is for constraint declaration/configuration errors, not runtime authorization failures — semantically wrong base; a plain RuntimeException or project-style exception would be cleaner.

Stale `throws KlawNotAuthorizedException` declarations remain on createTopicsCreateRequest/createTopicsUpdateRequest/createTopicDeleteRequest/getTopics paths although the checkIsAuthorized calls that threw it were removed.

Behavior change: deleteTopicRequests/deleteConnectorRequests used to return HTTP 200 with a NOT_AUTHORIZED body; now the aspect returns HTTP 401 — an API contract change for existing clients (documented by the new IT but not in the PR description).

Pointcut @annotation(permissionAllowed) only matches method-level annotations, so the TYPE target and @Inherited on @PermissionAllowed are dead capability; the null check on the bound annotation parameter is also redundant (it can never be null when bound).

Method name typo 'handlePermissionNotAuthoirzedExceptionInternal' (mirrors the pre-existing typo at line 31); unused @Autowired BuildProperties and wildcard imports added in TopicAclControllerIT are style nits.

points.point: Missing spring-boot-starter-aop dependency, aspect never woven, all checks silently disabled
points.note: Fabrication: spring-boot-starter-data-jpa (core/pom.xml line 61) transitively provides spring-boot-starter-aop under the Boot 3.2.0 parent; the PR's own ITs asserting 401 prove the aspect fires. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: Functional mismatch: annotation allows APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS OR APPROVE_CONNECTORS but service approve/decline still enforce only APPROVE_CONNECTORS
Redundant blocking service-layer checks remain (createConnectorRequest, restartConnector, createConnectorDeleteRequest, approve/decline), correctly excluding the filtering check at 532
Stale throws KlawNotAuthorizedException declarations on controller methods
Removed validator tests may need replacement / ensure coverage

missed: Approver-only permissions now gate requestor-facing /getTopicRequests, /topic/request/{topicReqId} and /getConnectorRequests (biggest real regression)
/createClaimTopicRequest left completely unprotected (no annotation, no service check)
@Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) on the annotation declaration is ineffective; belongs on the aspect class
New exception handler missing log.error, inconsistent with every other handler
Human reference point: checkPermissions signature cleanup in PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor
exceeds: OR-logic mismatch between annotation and residual APPROVE_CONNECTORS-only service checks
Inventory of redundant residual service-layer checks with correct filtering-check exclusion

points.point: CRITICAL: missing AOP dependency, aspect silently ignored
points.note: Same fabrication as v5; data-jpa transitively provides AOP and the PR's ITs prove the aspect fires. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: ConstraintDeclarationException misuse as parent class; overlaps existing KlawNotAuthorizedException handler

points.note: Verified unannotated; legitimate coverage gap given the PR's uniformity goal.
Real and important (one of the PR's biggest gaps), though the suggested VIEW_TOPICS permission is questionable vs the connector counterpart's REQUEST_CREATE.
False: the diff explicitly adds @PermissionAllowed to both endpoints. v7 inverted the real issue (they ARE annotated — with approver-only permissions that lock out requestors). Absence-fabrication.

points.point: Validator permission check removed; conditional hedge on AOP dependency; ensure IT coverage
superAdmin-unauthorized test intent should be verified/clarified
Other controllers (Schema/Acl/etc.) not annotated; suggest scoping note

missed: Approver-only permissions gating requestor-facing endpoints — v7 not only missed it but asserted the opposite (that those endpoints are unannotated)
@Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) on the annotation declaration is ineffective
Residual service-layer checks / OR-logic mismatch in KafkaConnectControllerService (approve/decline still APPROVE_CONNECTORS-only)
Human reference point: checkPermissions signature cleanup in PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor
exceeds: Stale throws declarations with accurate line numbers

qualitative: Both candidates anchor their reviews on the same fabricated blocker — a 'missing' spring-boot-starter-aop dependency that is in fact transitively present via spring-boot-starter-data-jpa under the Boot 3.2.0 parent, disproven by the PR's own ITs asserting 401. v5 is the stronger review on substance: its OR-logic mismatch and residual-service-check findings in KafkaConnectControllerService are verified real and valuable, but it misses every endpoint-coverage issue including the PR's biggest regression (approver-only permissions on requestor-facing list endpoints) and the unprotected /createClaimTopicRequest. v7 commendably catches /createClaimTopicRequest and /getTopicTeam, but commits a second absence-fabrication by claiming /getTopicRequests and /topic/request/{id} are unannotated when the diff plainly annotates them — inverting the actual bug — and pads with low-value scope/hedge points. Neither matched the human's aspect-signature suggestion, and neither caught the ineffective @Order placement or the unlogged exception handler.
